title: pittosporum spinescens
intro: Pittosporum spinescens, also known as the Australian cherry or black daphne, is a flowering plant native to the eastern coast of Australia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The plant's leaves and bark have been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, coughs, and skin conditions, offering health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting infections. Pittosporum spinescens can be prepared into herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
